description: Manual graph seed for Phase 1 MVP benchmark. Ingest this into Neptune or your graph store before running evaluations.

properties.name: order-management-service
properties.description: Core microservice for processing and managing customer orders. Handles order lifecycle, Stripe payment webhooks, and RabbitMQ event publishing.

properties.name: frontend-app
properties.description: Frontend application that integrates with the order-management-service backend API.

properties.name: auth.md
properties.summary: JWT authentication format, Authorization header, token claims (sub, role), 401 vs 403 error responses, correlation ID header.

properties.name: orders-api.md
properties.summary: REST API for creating and retrieving orders. POST /api/orders request body, response shape, 201 status. Server-side totalAmount calculation.

properties.name: order-statuses.md
properties.summary: Four order status values: PENDING, PAID, SHIPPED, CANCELLED. Status transition triggers. Eventual consistency warning after order creation.

properties.name: webhooks.md
properties.summary: Stripe webhook endpoint POST /api/webhooks/stripe. Raw body requirement, stripe-signature header, handled events, orderId metadata requirement for payment linking.

properties.name: events.md
properties.summary: RabbitMQ event publishing. Exchange name order_events, type topic. Routing key order.created. Fire-and-forget publishing, no guarantee of downstream side effects.

properties.name: error-handling.md
properties.summary: HTTP status codes, error response shape {error: string}, webhook errors return plain text not JSON. Floating point pricing in dollars, Stripe uses cents. MongoDB non-ACID caveat.

properties.name: pagination.md
properties.summary: Cursor-based pagination. Query params: limit (max 100), cursor, status filter, customerId filter. Role-based restriction: customerId filter requires admin role or matching sub claim.

properties.name: 001-database-choice.md
properties.summary: ADR: Chose MongoDB for flexible order schemas. Accepted. Consequence: no native ACID across documents, requires application-level two-phase commits.

properties.name: 002-async-messaging.md
properties.summary: ADR: Chose RabbitMQ for async event-driven architecture. Accepted. OrderCreated event published to topic exchange. Consequence: eventual consistency, may need saga pattern.

properties.description: Frontend application depends on the order-management-service REST API
Pagination doc references orders-api for the base endpoint path and auth requirements
Pagination doc references auth for role-based customerId filter restriction
Error handling doc references auth for 401/403 distinction
Error handling doc references MongoDB ADR for non-ACID caveat
Orders API doc references order statuses for status field values
Orders API doc references pagination for list endpoint query params
Orders API doc references error handling for floating point pricing note
Order statuses doc references webhooks for PENDING->PAID transition mechanism
Order statuses doc references events for eventual consistency caveat
Events doc is the operational implementation of the async messaging ADR
